abstract: Create an illusion of multiple flat layers by causing the article body to overlap the header as the user scrolls.
overview:
## Overview

**On this page, you’ll learn how to add parallax behavior to your article.**

You’ll first divide the article’s components into two sections. This requires moving a large amount of code. Then, you’ll simply add a `behavior` property on the upper section and complete the effect by giving the lower section an opaque background color.





### Divide the Article Content into Sections

Add sections to the article’s top-level `components` array and move all existing components into the new sections. This requires moving a large amount of code.

Your code should look like the example code [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-parallax-behavior#Section-Components-Result-Collapsed] and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-parallax-behavior#Section-Components-Result-Expanded].

After you make these changes in your code, you can successfully preview your working `article.json` file in News Preview, but it won’t look any different until you add parallax behavior in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-parallax-behavior#Add-Parallax-Behavior-in-the-First-Section].

#### Section Components: Copy This Code

#### Section Components: Result (Collapsed)

Ellipses (`...`) indicate lines of code that have been omitted from this example.

#### Section Components: Result (Expanded)

Ellipses (`...`) indicate lines of code that have been omitted from this example.

### Add Parallax Behavior in the First Section

Your article currently has two sections. You’ll use the first section’s `behavior` property to assign a parallax factor lower than 1, causing the first section to scroll more slowly than the default scroll speed.

Your code should look like the example code [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-parallax-behavior#Behavior-Result].

After you make this change in your code, you can preview your working `article.json` file in News Preview to see the changes in scroll speed. You might notice text becoming illegible as it covers the image. You’ll fix this in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-parallax-behavior#Use-the-Opaque-Background-with-the-Lower-Section] by adding a background color to the lower section.

#### Behavior: Copy This Code

#### Behavior: Result

Ellipses (`...`) indicate lines of code that have been omitted from this example.

### Define a ComponentStyle Object for Background Color

To finish the parallax behavior, you need to add a background color to the lower section. Before you can do that, you must define a `ComponentStyle` object.

Your code should look like the example code [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-parallax-behavior#bodyBackgroundStyle-Result].

#### bodyBackgroundStyle: Copy This Code

#### bodyBackgroundStyle: Result

Ellipses (`...`) indicate lines of code that have been omitted from this example.

### Use the Opaque Background with the Lower Section

As a final step, you need to add a reference to the `ComponentStyle` object you just defined.

To add the reference, in your `article.json` file, inside your second `section` component, add a property named `style` with a value of `"bodyBackgroundStyle"`.

Your code should look like the example code [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-parallax-behavior#Opaque-Background-Result].

After you make these changes in your code, you can preview your working `article.json` file in News Preview to see that the lower section remains legible as it scrolls past the upper section.

#### Opaque Background: Result

Ellipses (`...`) indicate lines of code that have been omitted from this example.

### Previous

[doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/creating-a-layered-header]

### Next

[doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/viewing-the-finished-article-for-advanced-design-tutorial-1]
